methodology: Mal CRAM · CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 (STR/SAR Guidance) · Thematic Review STR Framework (Jan 2023) · FATF Rec. 20/29 · FinCEN BSA (US BaaS)

templates.title: Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) — UAE FIU / goAML
templates.purpose: Report suspicious executed transactions related to ML, TF, or illegal organisations — no minimum threshold
templates.trigger: Suspicion confirmed after investigation; MLRO approval to file within SLA
templates.submittedTo: UAE Financial Intelligence Unit via goAML
templates.frequency: Ad hoc — max 35 business days from alert; 24 hours if expedited
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 · AML-CFT Law Art. 15 · Cabinet Decision 10/2019 · Mal AML Policy §14
templates.cramRef: Investigation case · OVR-010 · cram_ref · Mohsen narrative pack
checklist: goAML Report Cover mandatory fields complete (RFR, summary, action taken, location)
RFR codes match actual suspicion — not a defensive filing
Transaction tab populated with dates, amounts, counterparties, jurisdictions
Party types mapped (Person / Account / Entity) with full identifying details
Mohsen investigation complete · Vital4 screening disposition attached
KYC/CDD, account opening, receipts, financial statements attached where available
Maker-checker sign-off · No tipping-off · MLRO registered in goAML
Business days from alert ≤35 (or ≤24h expedited) documented

templates.title: Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) — UAE FIU / goAML
templates.purpose: Report non-transaction suspicious activity OR attempted/non-executed transactions (distinct from STR)
templates.trigger: Suspicion of activity without executed transactions, or blocked/rejected payment attempt
templates.submittedTo: UAE Financial Intelligence Unit via goAML
templates.frequency: Ad hoc — max 35 business days from alert; 24 hours if expedited
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §3.2 · Guidance: attempted transactions → SAR not STR
checklist: Report Type = SAR (not STR) — no executed suspicious transactions OR attempted/blocked activity
goAML Activity tab populated (not Transaction tab)
Location of Incident and RFR codes complete
Narrative covers Who/What/When/Where/Why/How for the suspicious activity
If attempted transaction: outcome (blocked/rejected) and block reason documented

templates.title: Suspicious Activity Report (SAR) — FinCEN BSA E-File
templates.purpose: Report suspicious activity for US BaaS customers under FinCEN requirements
templates.trigger: Suspicion identified; SAR committee / MLRO approval
templates.frequency: Ad hoc — within 30 calendar days of initial detection (FinCEN)
templates.policyRef: FinCEN SAR regulations · Mal US BaaS AML Program

templates.title: Attempted Transaction — UAE SAR (goAML)
templates.purpose: Report suspicious attempted but non-executed transactions — file as SAR per CBUAE §3.2 (not STR)
templates.trigger: Blocked / rejected suspicious payment attempt after investigation
templates.submittedTo: UAE FIU via goAML (Report Type: SAR)
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §3.2 · Attempted transactions → SAR · Mal AML Policy §14.3
checklist: Use RPT-SAR-UAE-001 structure — Report Type SAR not STR
Activity tab documents attempted transaction with block/reject outcome
RFR codes and narrative explain why attempt was suspicious
No tipping-off · MLRO approval within SLA

templates.title: Terrorist Property Report — UAE
templates.purpose: Report frozen or identified terrorist assets
templates.trigger: Terrorist financing match or designated person/asset identified
templates.submittedTo: UAE FIU · UAE Executive Office for Control & Non-Proliferation (sanctions)
templates.policyRef: UAE AML Law · UNSCR / UAE Local Terrorist Lists

templates.title: Sanctions Freeze Report — UAE competent authority
templates.purpose: Notify authorities that sanctioned assets have been frozen
templates.trigger: Confirmed sanctions true match (Vital4 disposition)
templates.submittedTo: UAE Executive Office for Control & Non-Proliferation · UAE FIU
templates.policyRef: OVR-001 · UAE Targeted Financial Sanctions

templates.title: OFAC Block Report — US BaaS
templates.purpose: Report blocked property under OFAC regulations
templates.trigger: OFAC SDN / sectoral sanctions true match
templates.submittedTo: OFAC · FinCEN (as applicable) · Partner sponsor bank
templates.policyRef: 31 CFR Chapter V · Mal US Sanctions Policy

templates.title: Currency Transaction Report (CTR) — FinCEN
templates.purpose: Report cash transactions exceeding USD 10,000 aggregate (US regime)
templates.trigger: Cash-in or cash-out ≥ USD 10,000 in single business day
templates.policyRef: FinCEN CTR requirements · 31 USC 5313

templates.title: Large Cash Transaction Report — UAE (where applicable)
templates.purpose: Report cash transactions above regulatory threshold
templates.trigger: Cash transaction exceeds CBUAE / policy threshold

templates.title: Cross-Border Currency Report
templates.purpose: Declare movement of cash across borders where required
templates.trigger: Cash import/export above declaration threshold

templates.title: Additional Information File (AIF) — goAML supplement
templates.purpose: Provide supplementary non-transactional information to FIU for a prior STR/SAR
templates.trigger: FIU requests additional information via goAML Message Board
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §3.2 · Supplementary report types

templates.title: Additional Information File with Transactions (AIFT) — goAML
templates.purpose: Provide supplementary transactional information to FIU for a prior STR/SAR
templates.trigger: FIU requests additional transactional detail via goAML Message Board
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §3.2
checklist: Transaction tab populated with requested transactions

templates.title: Request for Information response (RFI) — goAML
templates.purpose: Respond to FIU broadcast RFI without transactions via goAML
templates.trigger: FIU issues RFI to multiple LFIs via goAML
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §3.2 · RFI report type

templates.title: Request for Information with Transactions (RFIT) — goAML
templates.purpose: Respond to FIU broadcast RFI including transactional data via goAML
templates.trigger: FIU issues RFIT requiring transaction information
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §3.2 · RFIT report type

templates.title: High Risk Country Transaction Report (HRC) — goAML
templates.purpose: Report transactions linked to NAMLCFTC-designated high-risk countries
templates.trigger: Transaction involves high-risk country per UAE NAMLCFTC list
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §3.2 · NAMLCFTC high-risk countries

templates.title: High Risk Country Activity Report (HRCA) — goAML
templates.purpose: Report non-transaction activity linked to NAMLCFTC high-risk countries
templates.trigger: Customer activity involves high-risk country without reportable transactions
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §3.2

templates.title: Sanctions Match Notification — Regulator
templates.purpose: Notify prudential regulator of confirmed sanctions match
templates.trigger: Confirmed screening hit after Vital4 disposition

templates.title: FIU filing — draft workspace cover sheet (goAML maker-checker)
templates.purpose: Internal cover sheet before MLRO submits to FIU — validates goAML mandatory fields and CBUAE narrative quality
templates.trigger: Report ready for MLRO review
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 · Thematic Review STR Framework
checklist: goAML Report Cover mandatory fields complete
RFR codes match suspicion — not defensive filing
Business days from alert within SLA (35 std / 24h expedited)
templates.body:
FIU FILING — DRAFT COVER SHEET (CBUAE-aligned)
Guidance: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 · Thematic Review STR (Jan 2023)

Report type: {{reportType}} (STR / SAR / AIF / AIFT / RFI / RFIT / HRC / HRCA)
Target system: {{fiuSystem}} (goAML / FinCEN BSA E-File)
Internal ref: {{reportRef}}
Case linkage: {{caseId}} · CRAM: {{cramRef}}

── goAML MANDATORY COVER CHECK ──
□ Description/Summary of Report  □ RFR codes  □ Action Taken
□ Location of Incident (STR/SAR)  □ MLRO details match registration

── NARRATIVE QUALITY (Annex 1 anti-patterns) ──
□ Subject identifying details complete (not partial)
□ Specific transaction dates/amounts (not aggregates only)
□ Counterparty names, banks, accounts, jurisdictions
□ Why suspicious for this customer (expected vs observed)
□ Party relationships explained  □ Not a defensive filing

── EVIDENCE ──
□ Investigation narrative (Mohsen)  □ Transaction/activity schedule
□ CDD/EDD summary  □ Vital4 screening disposition
□ KYC, IDs, account opening, receipts (where available)
□ Tipping-off checklist complete

── SLA ──
Alert date: {{alertDate}} · Business days elapsed: {{businessDaysElapsed}}
Target filing: {{targetFileDate}} (max 35 business days / 24h expedited)
Complex investigation: □ Initial (day 15) □ Follow-up (day 45)

Drafted by: Jana (maker) · Reviewed by: {{checkerName}} (checker)
Approved by MLRO: _____________ · FIU ref (post-submit): {{fiuRef}}

templates.title: FIU Request for Information — response template
templates.purpose: Structured response to FIU or regulator information request
templates.trigger: RFI received from FIU / CBUAE / FinCEN

templates.title: FIU submission status tracker
templates.purpose: Track draft → submitted → acknowledged → feedback
templates.trigger: Any FIU filing
templates.body:
FIU SUBMISSION STATUS — {{reportRef}}

Report type: {{reportType}} (STR/SAR/AIF/AIFT/RFI/RFIT/HRC/HRCA)
Customer: {{customerName}} ({{customerId}})
Alert date: {{alertDate}} · SLA: 35 business days (std) / 24h (expedited)

| Stage | Date | Owner | Notes |
|-------|------|-------|-------|
| Alert raised (Oscilar/TM) | {{alertDate}} | System | Rule: {{oscilarRuleId}} |
| Investigation opened | {{investigationOpenDate}} | Mohsen | Case: {{caseId}} |
| Internal STR recommendation | {{internalStrDate}} | Analyst | Ref: WF-INT-STR-001 |
| Draft created (Jana) | {{draftDate}} | Jana | {{draftNotes}} |
| Maker-checker review | {{reviewDate}} | {{checkerName}} | {{reviewNotes}} |
| MLRO approval | {{mlroApprovalDate}} | {{mlroName}} | Business days: {{businessDaysToFile}} |
| Submitted to FIU (goAML) | {{submitDate}} | {{mlroName}} | FIU ref: {{fiuAckRef}} |
| Acknowledgement received | {{ackDate}} | — | |
| Post-STR actions initiated | {{postStrDate}} | Compliance | WF-POST-STR-001 |
| FIU feedback / AIF request | {{feedbackDate}} | — | {{feedbackSummary}} |

Retention: 5 years minimum (CBUAE guidance §5)

templates.title: Internal STR/SAR recommendation — analyst to MLRO
templates.purpose: Document analyst recommendation to file or not file before MLRO decision
templates.trigger: Investigation complete; suspicion assessment required
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 · Thematic Review — internal escalation
checklist: Decline rationale documented if not filing

templates.title: Alert disposition record — TM alert to case closure
templates.purpose: Document alert review, escalation, and closure rationale per Thematic Review expectations
templates.trigger: Every TM alert disposition (close, escalate, STR)
templates.policyRef: Thematic Review STR Framework · CBUAE TM expectations

templates.title: Post-STR action plan — CBUAE §6.2
templates.purpose: Document mandatory post-filing actions after STR/SAR submission
templates.trigger: Within 5 business days of FIU submission
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §6.2 · Thematic Review §2.6
checklist: Risk-based EDD and enhanced monitoring implemented
Retain vs exit documented with senior management approval

templates.title: Complex investigation STR — initial (day 15) and follow-up (day 45)
templates.purpose: File initial STR at day 15 and follow-up at day 45 for complex investigations per CBUAE §4.7
templates.trigger: Investigation exceeds standard timeline; complex case designation
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 §4.7 · 15 + 30 business day complex investigation

templates.title: Continuing activity STR — post-initial filing
templates.purpose: Report additional suspicious transactions after initial STR for same customer/activity
templates.trigger: New suspicious transactions identified after initial STR filed
templates.policyRef: CBUAE Notice 3354/2022 · Continuing suspicious activity

templates.title: Board FinCrime & AML pack
templates.purpose: Quarterly board reporting on financial crime posture
templates.trigger: Quarterly board cycle
templates.policyRef: Mal Board AML Charter · CBUAE corporate governance
templates.body:
BOARD FINANCIAL CRIME PACK — {{quarter}} {{year}}
Prepared by: Jana · Approved by: {{mlroName}}, MLRO

── EXECUTIVE SUMMARY ──
{{executiveSummary}}

── CRAM HEALTH ──
Overall health score: {{cramHealth}}%
Model version: {{modelVersion}} · Status: {{modelStatus}}
Open governance items: {{openItems}}

── KEY METRICS ──
| Metric | Current | Prior Q | Target |
|--------|---------|---------|--------|
| Compliance score | {{complianceScore}} | — | ≥85 |
| Control effectiveness | {{controlEffectiveness}} | — | ≥70% |
| High-risk customers | {{highRiskPct}}% | — | Within appetite |
| STR/SAR filed | {{strCount}} | — | — |
| STR avg business days (alert→file) | {{strAvgDays}} | — | ≤35 |
| STR SLA breaches | {{strSlaBreaches}} | — | 0 |
| Defensive filings (QA) | {{defensiveFilings}} | — | 0 |
| Post-STR EDD completion | {{postStrEddPct}}% | — | 100% |
| Manual vs system CRA variance | {{variance}}% | — | ≤0.1% |

── REGULATORY & RISK HIGHLIGHTS ──
{{regulatoryHighlights}}

── EMERGING TYPOLOGIES (Mohsen) ──
{{typologies}}

── OUTSTANDING ISSUES ──
{{outstandingIssues}}

── MLRO RECOMMENDATIONS ──
{{mlroRecommendations}}

Appendices: CRAM obligation coverage · Validation summary · Open investigations

templates.title: Regulatory examination pack — cover index
templates.purpose: Structured exam pack for CBUAE / US partner bank inspection
templates.trigger: Examination notice or proactive readiness
templates.body:
REGULATORY EXAMINATION PACK — INDEX
Exam reference: {{examRef}} · Sample size: {{sampleSize}} customers
Prepared: {{datePrepared}} · MLRO: {{mlroName}}
CBUAE STR framework evidence (Thematic Review Jan 2023)

CONTENTS (per customer sample {{customerId}}):
□ 1. Complete customer profile
□ 2. Customer risk assessment (CRAM) — rating {{craRating}} · assessment ID {{assessmentId}}
□ 3. CDD/EDD evidence file
□ 4. UBO information (if entity)
□ 5. Transaction history ({{txnPeriod}})
□ 6. Alert history (Oscilar + Vital4) with disposition records (WF-ALERT-DISP-001)
□ 7. Investigation notes & disposition
□ 8. STR/SAR history with goAML FIU references
□ 9. Internal STR recommendation (WF-INT-STR-001) and maker-checker sign-off
□ 10. STR narrative quality — Who/What/When/Where/Why/How
□ 11. Post-STR action plan (WF-POST-STR-001) — EDD, reclass, retain/exit
□ 12. Immutable audit trail (cram_ref linked)
□ 13. Policy version at time of decision
□ 14. Screening history (Vital4 authority)
□ 15. STR filing timeliness (business days alert→file, max 35)
□ 16. Model validation summary (SR 11-7)

Generation target: < 2 hours per Mal platform architecture spec.

templates.title: Email — Missing KYC documents request
templates.purpose: Professional customer communication requesting missing CDD documents
templates.trigger: KYC incomplete · onboarding blocked · periodic review
templates.body:
Subject: Action required — Please provide additional verification documents | Mal Bank

Dear {{customerName}},

Thank you for choosing Mal Bank. To complete your account verification and comply with regulatory requirements (Central Bank of the UAE / applicable US banking regulations), we need the following document(s):

{{missingDocumentsList}}

Please upload these securely via the Mal app by {{deadlineDate}}. If we do not receive them by this date, we may need to restrict account activity until verification is complete.

Why we need this:
Mal Bank is required under AML/CFT regulations to verify the identity of all customers and, where applicable, the source of funds. This protects you and the financial system.

How to submit:
1. Open the Mal app → Profile → Documents
2. Upload clear, colour copies (PDF or photo)
3. Ensure documents are valid and not expired

If you have questions, contact us at {{supportEmail}} or {{supportPhone}}. Please do not send documents via unsecured email.

Kind regards,
{{senderName}}
Customer Due Diligence Team
Mal Bank

── Internal note ──
Customer ID: {{customerId}} · CRA: {{craRating}} · DQ gate: BLOCKED until received · Retention: per AML policy

templates.title: Email — Source of funds / wealth clarification
templates.purpose: Request SoF/SoW documentation for EDD or investigation
templates.trigger: TM alert · profile deviation · high-value transaction · MLRO request
templates.body:
Subject: Information request — Source of funds / wealth | Mal Bank

Dear {{customerName}},

As part of our regulatory obligations to safeguard the financial system, we are reviewing activity on your account (ref: {{accountRef}}).

We kindly request the following information to help us understand the source of funds/wealth related to {{transactionOrActivity}}:

{{informationRequested}}

Examples of acceptable evidence:
• Salary slips / employment contract (last 3 months)
• Bank statements showing salary credits
• Business financial statements / tax returns
• Sale agreement (property, investments)
• Inheritance / gift documentation

Please provide this by {{deadlineDate}} via the secure document upload in the Mal app.

We appreciate your cooperation. Unusual delays may result in temporary restrictions on certain transactions, in line with our AML policy and regulatory requirements.

Kind regards,
{{senderName}}
Financial Crime Compliance
Mal Bank

── Internal ──
Case: {{caseId}} · Mohsen ref: {{investigationRef}} · Do NOT disclose investigation status (tipping-off)

templates.title: Email — Enhanced Due Diligence information request
templates.purpose: Formal EDD request for high-risk / PEP / complex customers
templates.trigger: CRA High · PEP · complex UBO · MLRO EDD workflow

templates.title: Email — Periodic review overdue reminder
templates.purpose: Remind customer to complete periodic KYC refresh
templates.trigger: Periodic review due date passed
templates.body:
Subject: Reminder — Please complete your account review | Mal Bank

Dear {{customerName}},

Our records show your periodic account review is overdue (due date: {{dueDate}}). Regulatory requirements oblige us to keep customer information up to date.

Please complete the review in the Mal app within {{graceDays}} days to avoid restrictions on your account.

Steps:
1. Open Mal app → Account Review
2. Confirm or update your personal/business details
3. Reconfirm source of funds if requested

Kind regards,
Mal Bank Customer Care
